name: KRATOS DEFENSE & SECURITY SOLUTIONS, INC.
business: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions, Inc. is a technology, hardware, products, system and software company addressing the defense, national security, and commercial markets. The company's primary business areas include virtualized ground systems for satellites, jet powered unmanned aerial drone systems, hypersonic vehicles and rocket systems, propulsion systems, C5ISR and microwave electronic products, and virtual & augmented reality training systems. Kratos primarily serves U.S. Government agencies, including the Department of War, intelligence agencies, and foreign governments, as well as commercial customers.
sellsTo: U.S. Government (all branches of U.S. military and FMS)
namedCustomers: Office of the Secretary of War

exposures.sentence: We believe that Kratos is an industry leader of high performance, jet powered unmanned aerial target drone systems.
Kratos is also a recognized industry leader in low cost hypersonic flight vehicles, ballistic missile targets and other systems.

We believe that Kratos is an industry leader in ground-based command, control and communications systems (C3), Telemetry Tracking and Control (TT&C) and other systems for satellites, and a leader in related radio frequency interference identification, geolocation and mitigation, or Space Domain Awareness.

Kratos primary business areas include, virtualized ground systems for satellites and space vehicles including software for command & control (C2) and telemetry, tracking and control (TT&C), jet powered unmanned aerial drone systems, hypersonic vehicles and rocket systems, propulsion systems for drones, missiles, loitering munitions, supersonic systems, space craft and launch systems, command, control, communication, computing, combat, intelligence surveillance and reconnaissance (C5ISR) and microwave electronic products for missile, radar, air defense, missile defense, space, satellite, counter unmanned aircraft systems (CUAS), directed energy, communication and other systems, and virtual & augmented reality training systems for the warfighter.

Kratos primary capabilities and areas of focus, certain ones which are listed below, are aligned with the objectives of the U.S. Government including those outlined in the 2022 National Defense Strategy document: ... Cybersecurity and information assurance.
Unmanned aerial drones, unmanned ground and unmanned seaborne systems and related autonomy and artificial intelligence.
Kratos is a recognized industry leader in the rapid development of low cost jet engines for missiles, drones and other systems.

Kratos is also a recognized industry leader in affordable rocket systems and SRMs which are specifically designed and developed for low cost ballistic missile target, hypersonic, sub orbital and other National Security related missions.
Kratos primary business areas include ... virtual & augmented reality training systems for the warfighter.

catalysts.event: Second successful flight of Erinyes hypersonic test bed
catalysts.sentence: The Company announced the second successful flight of the Erinyes hypersonic test bed in January 2025.

catalysts.event: Completion of PDR for SDA's AFCGI system
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, we completed the preliminary design review (PDR) for the Space Development Agency's Advanced Fire Control Ground Integration (AFCGI) system.

catalysts.event: Successful altitude testing of GEK800 engine
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, GE Aerospace and Kratos announced the successful completion of altitude testing on its GEK800 engine.

catalysts.event: Extinguishment of Term Loan A debt
catalysts.sentence: On July 2, 2025, we extinguished all outstanding Term Loan A debt under the 2022 Credit Facility.

catalysts.event: Enactment of One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA)
catalysts.sentence: On July 4, 2025, the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) was enacted.

On February 3, 2026 President Trump signed the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026 (H.R. 7148) a $1.2 trillion funding package, that ended a brief government shutdown that began on February 1, 2026.

catalysts.event: Ramp-up of Prometheus venture and BladeWorks facility
catalysts.sentence: Investments related to the Company's Prometheus venture with Rafael and the new BladeWorks turbofan production facility in Oklahoma related to our arrangement with GE Aerospace are expected to begin to ramp up during 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Total U.S. Federal Fiscal Year 2026 National Security spend approximately $1 trillion.
tamClaims.sentence: This $838.7 billion, plus the approximate $113 billion included in the OBBBA noted above, and including approximately $45 billion in Department of Energy National Security related funds, brings the total U.S. Federal Fiscal Year 2026 National Security spend to approximately $1 trillion.
